hints.ancillae: 'Ancillae' is a noun meaning 'of his handmaid'
hints.suae: 'Suae' is a possessive adjective meaning 'his/her own'

hints.to look back: While 'respicere' can mean 'look back', in this context it means 'to regard/look upon'

steps.reveal: respexit = he has regarded / he looked upon

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'respicit'; 'respexit' uses the perfect stem

hints.aspicio, aspicere, aspexi, aspectum: These are the principal parts of 'aspicio' (to look at)
hints.specto, spectare, spectavi, spectatum: These are the principal parts of 'specto' (to watch)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'video' (to see)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'humilitas' has genitive 'humilitatis'
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'humilitas' ends in -as with genitive -atis
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es with genitive -ei; 'humilitas' has genitive 'humilitatis'

hints.nominative: Nominative singular is 'ancilla'; 'ancillae' could be nominative plural but context shows possession
hints.dative: Dative singular would also be 'ancillae', but here it indicates possession
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address; 'ancillae' here is genitive of possession

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'ancilla' ends in -a
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'ancilla' has genitive 'ancillae'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive -us; 'ancilla' has genitive 'ancillae'

translation: He has regarded the lowliness of his handmaid
